current_surface.title: Agreeableness | Big Five guide
current_surface.description: Agreeableness describes stable tendencies around trust, cooperation, empathy, boundaries, conflict, and directness, helping explain learning, communication, and adaptation patterns.
current_surface.quick_answer: Agreeableness concerns trust, cooperation, empathy, boundaries, conflict, and directness. It is not a type label and does not rank ability.

faq.question: What does Agreeableness mainly describe?
faq.answer: Agreeableness describes tendencies around trust, cooperation, empathy, boundaries, conflict, and directness in choices and communication.

faq.question: Is higher or lower better?
faq.answer: No. Higher and lower are tendencies; usefulness depends on context.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: How can I observe this domain?
faq.answer: Watch responses to stress, feedback, change, and collaboration rather than one behavior.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Can it decide work choices?
faq.answer: No. It can provide preference clues, but cannot replace ability, experience, and opportunity.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: How does it relate to facets?
faq.answer: Facets are narrower layers inside the broad domain.

current_surface.title: Big Five Personality | Big Five guide
current_surface.description: The Big Five describes personality differences through five continuous dimensions: openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, and neuroticism.
current_surface.quick_answer: The Big Five is not a fixed personality type table. It describes stable tendencies through five continuous dimensions and supports self-understanding, communication, and learning reflection.

faq.question: Is Big Five a personality type system?
faq.answer: No. Big Five is a dimensional model; it does not sort people into fixed types.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: What does OCEAN mean?
faq.answer: OCEAN is a common abbreviation for the five domains.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Are higher or lower traits better?
faq.answer: No. High and low describe tendency strength, and usefulness depends on task, relationship, and environment.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Can Big Five decide a career?
faq.answer: No. It can describe preferences, but career choice also needs skills, experience, opportunity, and constraints.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: What are the 30 facets?
faq.answer: Facets are narrower explanations inside the five broad domains. This version provides an overview only.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: What does Emotional Stability mean?
faq.answer: Emotional Stability means tendencies around lower relative stress response, faster recovery, less sustained high arousal are more likely to appear in daily choices.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Is this better?
faq.answer: No. It is a tendency pattern; usefulness depends on context and regulation.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Why read the other pole?
faq.answer: The other pole shows needs you may miss, such as stability, exploration, boundaries, or recovery.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Can it decide what work fits me?
faq.answer: No. It can describe work-style preferences, but it cannot decide a career by itself.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: How can I use this practically?
faq.answer: Choose one situation, keep the strength of this pole, and add one small move from the other pole.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Why are there 30 facets?
faq.answer: Common Big Five explanations can divide each broad domain into narrower facets; this page uses a 5 x 6 overview structure.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Does this open facet detail pages?
faq.answer: No. This stage is overview-only and does not generate 30 facet detail pages.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Are facets 30 personality types?
faq.answer: No. They are narrower explanations inside dimensions, not personality types.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: How should I use facets?
faq.answer: Use them to make the five broad domains more precise, not to replace a full assessment.
faq.reason: Reuses reviewed public package FAQ intent while preserving Big Five method boundaries.

faq.question: Do facets have research support?
faq.answer: BFI-2 and other five-factor materials use hierarchical or facet language, although labels differ by instrument.
